Month-end close timetable Policy ID | POL_001 Policy section | Month-end close Policy owner | Priya Nair - Financial Controller Policy requirements Departmental accrual submissions are due by 12:00 noon on business day 2. citation: finance_policies_and_procedures.docx - section 4: 3. Accrual recognition and review
content_preview: 3. Accrual recognition and review Policy ID | POL_002 Policy section | Accruals Policy owner | Priya Nair - Financial Controller Policy requirements An accrual is required when goods or services have been received but the supplier invoice has not been recorded. The minimum accrual threshold is AUD 5,000 per individual item. Accruals must be supported by a purchase order, contract, supplier estimate or other reasonable calculation. Accruals must be reviewed each month and reversed when the related invoice is recorded.

4. Prepayment recognition Policy ID | POL_003 Policy section | Prepayments Policy owner | Priya Nair - Financial Controller Policy requirements A payment must be recorded as a prepayment when it is at least AUD 12,000 and the benefit extends for more than three months. Prepayments must be amortised monthly over the period in which the economic benefit is received. The supporting schedule must show the supplier, total amount, service period and monthly release.

5. Operating expenditure approval Policy ID | POL_004 Policy section | Operating expenditure Policy owner | Olivia Bennett - Finance Director Approval levels Minimum amount | Maximum amount | Required approvers AUD 0 | AUD 5,000 | Department Manager AUD 5,001 | AUD 25,000 | Department Director AUD 25,001 | AUD 100,000 | Department Director; Finance Director AUD 100,001 | No upper limit | Finance Director; Chief Executive Officer

6. Purchase order requirements Policy ID | POL_005 Policy section | Procurement Policy owner | Olivia Bennett - Finance Director Policy requirements A purchase order must be approved before committing Harbour Retail Group to goods or services above AUD 5,000. Splitting a purchase into smaller amounts to avoid the threshold is prohibited. Payroll, taxes, rent, regulated utilities and emergency expenditure approved by the Finance Director are exempt. Invoices without a valid purchase order may be returned to the requesting department.

7. Capital expenditure approval Policy ID | POL_006 Policy section | Capital expenditure Policy owner | Olivia Bennett - Finance Director Approval levels Minimum amount | Maximum amount | Required approvers AUD 0 | AUD 50,000 | Department Director; Finance Director AUD 50,001 | AUD 250,000 | Finance Director; Chief Executive Officer AUD 250,001 | No upper limit | Finance Director; Chief Executive Officer; Board of Directors Additional requirements Capital expenditure above AUD 50,000 requires a documented business case. The business case must include expected benefits, implementation cost, timing, risks and financial return. Projects may not be divided into smaller components to avoid an approval threshold.

Manual journal preparation and approval Policy ID | POL_007 Policy section | Manual journals Policy owner | Priya Nair - Financial Controller Policy requirements Every manual journal must include a clear description, calculation and supporting documentation. The preparer and approver must be different people. A journal preparer may not approve their own journal. Journals above AUD 100,000 require approval from the Financial Controller.

citation: finance_policies_and_procedures.docx - section 10: 9. Balance sheet reconciliation timetable
content_preview: 9. Balance sheet reconciliation timetable Policy ID | POL_008 Policy section | Balance sheet reconciliations Policy owner | Priya Nair - Financial Controller Policy requirements All balance sheet accounts must be reconciled monthly. Preparers must complete reconciliations by business day 7. Reviewers must complete their review by business day 10. Reconciling items older than 60 days must include an owner and a documented resolution date.

10. Finance document retention Policy ID | POL_009 Policy section | Document retention Policy owner | Priya Nair - Financial Controller Policy requirements Accounting records and supporting documents must be retained for seven years. Electronic records must be stored in an approved company system. Documents subject to an audit, investigation or legal hold must not be destroyed.

11. Departmental forecast submissions Policy ID | POL_010 Policy section | Forecasting Policy owner | Daniel Wu - Head of FP&A Policy requirements Department Directors are responsible for the completeness and reasonableness of their forecasts. Forecast submissions must explain material changes to revenue, expenditure, headcount and capital expenditure. Material forecast movements above AUD 100,000 require written commentary. The Head of FP&A consolidates and challenges departmental submissions before executive review.

12. Appendix - key thresholds Control | Threshold | Requirement Accrual | AUD 5,000 | Minimum individual accrual threshold Prepayment | AUD 12,000 | Must also provide benefit for more than three months Purchase order | Above AUD 5,000 | Must be approved before committing expenditure Manual journal | Above AUD 100,000 | Requires Financial Controller approval Capex business case | Above AUD 50,000 | Documented business case required Board capex approval | Above AUD 250,000 | Board of Directors approval required Forecast commentary | Above AUD 100,000 | Written commentary required Record retention | Seven years | Accounting records and supporting documentation End of Finance Policies and Procedures

KPI: Revenue. Category: Growth. Definition: Total income generated from the sale of goods before deducting operating expenses. Formula: Gross sales less returns, discounts and sales taxes. Frequency: Monthly. Owner: Daniel Wu, Head of FP&A. Data source: ERP general ledger and point-of-sale system. Unit: AUD. Better direction: Higher. Target: period FY2025/26, operator >=, value 124000000.

citation: finance_kpi_dictionary.json - KPI KPI_FIN_002: Like-for-like sales growth
content_preview: KPI: Like-for-like sales growth. Category: Growth. Definition: Percentage change in sales from comparable stores that traded in both the current and prior periods. Formula: (Comparable store sales current period - comparable store sales prior period) / comparable store sales prior period. Frequency: Monthly. Owner: James Carter, Retail Operations Director. Data source: Point-of-sale system. Unit: percentage. Better direction: Higher. Target: period FY2025/26, operator >=, value 0.03.

KPI: Gross margin. Category: Profitability. Definition: Gross profit expressed as a percentage of revenue. Formula: (Revenue - cost of goods sold) / revenue. Frequency: Monthly. Owner: Daniel Wu, Head of FP&A. Data source: ERP general ledger. Unit: percentage. Better direction: Higher. Target: period Nine months ended 31 March 2026, operator >=, value 0.431.

KPI: Adjusted EBITDA. Category: Profitability. Definition: Earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation, adjusted for approved non-recurring items. Formula: Operating profit + depreciation + amortisation + approved adjustments. Frequency: Monthly. Owner: Olivia Bennett, Finance Director. Data source: ERP general ledger and FP&A adjustment schedule. Unit: AUD. Better direction: Higher. Target: period FY2025/26, operator >=, value 12200000.

citation: finance_kpi_dictionary.json - KPI KPI_FIN_005: Operating expense ratio
content_preview: KPI: Operating expense ratio. Category: Cost efficiency. Definition: Operating expenses expressed as a percentage of revenue. Formula: Operating expenses / revenue. Frequency: Monthly. Owner: Daniel Wu, Head of FP&A. Data source: ERP general ledger. Unit: percentage. Better direction: Lower. Target: period FY2025/26, operator <=, value 0.326.

KPI: Inventory days. Category: Working capital. Definition: Estimated number of days that inventory is held before being sold. Formula: Average inventory / annualised cost of goods sold * 365. Frequency: Monthly. Owner: Liam O'Connor, Supply Chain Director. Data source: ERP inventory and general ledger modules. Unit: days. Better direction: Lower. Target: period FY2025/26, operator <=, value 92.

KPI: Stock availability. Category: Operations. Definition: Percentage of active products available for sale when measured. Formula: Available active product locations / total active product locations. Frequency: Weekly. Owner: James Carter, Retail Operations Director. Data source: Inventory management system. Unit: percentage. Better direction: Higher. Target: period FY2025/26, operator >=, value 0.96.

content_preview: KPI: Online conversion rate. Category: E-commerce. Definition: Percentage of website sessions that result in a completed customer order. Formula: Completed online orders / website sessions. Frequency: Weekly. Owner: Mia Thompson, E-commerce Director. Data source: E-commerce platform and web analytics system. Unit: percentage. Better direction: Higher. Target: period FY2025/26, operator >=, value 0.032.

content_preview: KPI: Average transaction value. Category: Sales productivity. Definition: Average revenue generated by each completed customer transaction.
